input.raw_text_snippet:
107. Numerical taxonomy; 
 
(1) doesn’t involve the use of computers. 
 
(2) is based on all observable characteristics. 
 
(3) enables scientists to consider only one character 
at a time. 
 
(4) uses the chemical constituents of the plant to 
resolve confusions.

output.text: Numerical taxonomy;

options.text: doesn’t involve the use of computers.
is based on all observable characteristics.
enables scientists to consider only one character at a time.
uses the chemical constituents of the plant to resolve confusions.

output.explanation:
Numerical Taxonomy which is now easily carried out using computers is based on all observable characteristics. Number and codes are assigned to all the characters and the data are then processed. In this way each character is given equal importance and at the same time hundreds of characters can be considered. Cytotaxonomy, that is based on cytological information like chromosome number, structure, behaviour and chemotaxonomy, that uses the chemical constituents of the plant to resolve confusions, are also used by taxonomists these days.
(NCERT Ed. 2025-26, 11th, Page No. 24)

110. Given below are two statements: one is labelled as 
Assertion A and the other is labelled as Reason R. 
 
Assertion (A): At any point the rate of 
photosynthesis will be determined by the factor 
available at sub-optimal levels. 
 
Reason (R): Though several factors interact and 
simultaneously affect photosynthesis or CO2 
fixation, usually one factor is the major cause or is 
the one that limits the rate. 
 
In the light of the above statements, choose the 
correct answer from the options given below: 
 
(1) Both A and R are true, and R is the correct 
explanation of A. 
 
(2) Both A and R are true, but R is not the correct 
explanation of A. 
 
(3) A is true, but R is false. 
 
(4) A is false, but R is true.

output.text:
Given below are two statements: one is labelled as Assertion A and the other is labelled as Reason R.

Assertion (A): At any point the rate of photosynthesis will be determined by the factor available at sub-optimal levels.

Reason (R): Though several factors interact and simultaneously affect photosynthesis or CO₂ fixation, usually one factor is the major cause or is the one that limits the rate.

In the light of the above statements, choose the correct answer from the options given below:

options.text: Both A and R are true, and R is the correct explanation of A.
Both A and R are true, but R is not the correct explanation of A.
A is true, but R is false.
A is false, but R is true.

output.explanation: At any point the rate of photosynthesis will be determined by the factor available at sub-optimal levels. Though several factors interact and simultaneously affect photosynthesis or CO₂ fixation, usually one factor is the major cause or is the one that limits the rate.

140. Which of the following statements is false? 
 
(1) Analysis of urine helps in clinical diagnosis of 
many 
metabolic 
disorders 
as 
well 
as 
malfunctioning of the kidney.  
 
(2) The process of release of urine is called 
micturition.  
 
(3) An adult human excretes, on an average, 1 to 
1.5 litres of urine per hour.  
 
(4) On an average, 25-30 gm of urea is excreted out 
per day.

output.text: Which of the following statements is false?

options.text: Analysis of urine helps in clinical diagnosis of many metabolic disorders as well as malfunctioning of the kidney.
The process of release of urine is called micturition.
An adult human excretes, on an average, 1 to 1.5 litres of urine per hour.
On an average, 25-30 gm of urea is excreted out per day.

output.explanation: Urinalysis helps detect metabolic disorders (e.g. diabetes mellitus via glucose in urine) and kidney problems (e.g. proteinuria). Micturition is the scientific term for the act of passing urine. An adult human excretes about 1 to 1.5 litres of urine per day, not per hour. (NCERT Ed. 2025-26, 11th, Page No. 213)

output.text: What was the name of the ship on which Charles Darwin made his sea voyage around the world?
output.explanation:
• This is a direct factual question from the introduction of the Evolution chapter.
• Charles Darwin set out on a sea voyage around the world on a ship named H.M.S. Beagle.
• Based on observations made during this voyage, he concluded that existing living forms share similarities to varying degrees not only among themselves but also with life forms that existed millions of years ago.

output.text:
Given below are two statements.

Statement I: A motor neuron along with the muscle fibres connected to it constitute a motor unit.

Statement II: In the sliding filament theory of muscle contraction, the A-band width remains constant while the I-band and H-zone shorten.

In the light of the above statements, choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below:

options.text: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct.
Statement I is incorrect but Statement II is correct.
Statement I is correct but Statement II is incorrect.
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect.

output.explanation:
• A motor neuron along with the muscle fibres connected to it constitute a motor unit.
• The A-band is defined by the length of the thick myosin filaments. Muscle contraction is due to the sliding of the thin filaments past the thick filaments, not a shortening of the filaments themselves. Therefore, the A-band length remains constant, and the Reason (R) is the precise explanation for the constant width of the A-band.
(NCERT Ed. 2025-26, 11th, Page No. 224)

output.text: A particle executing S.H.M. has velocities v₁ and v₂ at distance x₁ and x₂ respectively from mean position. The angular velocity (ω) of the particle is given by
output.explanation:
In S.H.M velocities of a particle at distance x₁ and x₂ from mean position.
v₁ = ω√(A² − x₁²) ....(i)
Similarly,
v₂ = ω√(A² − x₂²) ....(ii)
Subtracting (ii) from equation (i), we get—
v₁² − v₂² = ω²(A² − x₁²) − ω²(A² − x₂²)
v₁² − v₂² = ω²(x₂² − x₁²)
ω² = (v₁² − v₂²)/(x₂² − x₁²)
ω = √((v₁² − v₂²)/(x₂² − x₁²))
⇒ ω = √((v₁² − v₂²)/(x₂² − x₁²))

(NCERT Ed. 2025-26, 11th, Page No. 268)

options.text: Kidneys are reddish brown and bean shaped.
Kidneys are situated between the levels of last thoracic and third lumbar vertebra.
In humans, the excretory system consists of one pair each of kidney, urethra, urinary bladder and ureter.
Each kidney of an adult human measures 10-12 cm in length and 5-7 cm in width.

output.explanation: The human kidneys are indeed typically reddish-brown in color and have a distinct bean shape. The kidneys are retroperitoneal organs located on either side of the vertebral column. Their position generally spans from the level of the last thoracic vertebra (T₁₂) down to the third lumbar vertebra (L₃). The right kidney is often slightly lower due to the liver's position. The components of the human urinary (excretory) system: Kidneys: There are one pair (two) of kidneys. Ureters: There are one pair (two) of ureters, one draining each kidney. Urinary Bladder: There is one urinary bladder, not a pair. Urethra: There is one urethra, not a pair.

30. 
A cracker is thrown in air with speed 20 m s–1 at 
angle 45° with the horizontal. At a height 1 m from 
the ground, it explodes into a number of fragments 
which follow different parabolic paths. What is the 
speed of centre of mass of all fragments, when it is 
at a height 2 m from the ground? (g = 10 m s–2) 
 
(1) 6 10 m s–1 
(2) 6 5 m s–1 
 
(3) 4 10 m s–1 
(4) 6 3 m s–1

output.text: A cracker is thrown in air with speed 20 m s⁻¹ at angle 45° with the horizontal. At a height 1 m from the ground, it explodes into a number of fragments which follow different parabolic paths. What is the speed of centre of mass of all fragments, when it is at a height 2 m from the ground? (g = 10 m s⁻²)
output.explanation: Explosion is internal ⇒ CM follows the same projectile path as if no explosion. ½ m × (20)² + 0 = ½ mv₂² + mg(2). 400/2 = (v₂)²/2 + 20. v₂ = √360 m/s.

output.explanation: Acceleration-time graph shows acceleration 10 m/s² for 2 s, then linearly decreases to 0 at 6 s. Velocity change = area under acceleration-time graph. Initial velocity zero, velocity becomes zero again when net area is zero. Calculate time accordingly to get 11.65 s.

output.text: Two bulbs rated (25W, 220V) and (100W, 220V) are connected in series to a 440V supply. Which bulb will fuse?
output.explanation:
R₂₅ = 220² / 25, R₁₀₀ = 220² / 100
R₂₅ = 4R₁₀₀
Total R = 5R₁₀₀
Current, I = 440 / 5R₁₀₀
Voltage across 25 W bulb:
V₂₅ = I × R₂₅ = (440 / 5R₁₀₀) × R₂₅ = 440 × 4/5 = 352 V
Since, 352 V > 220 V (rated), the 25 W bulb fuses.
Also V₁₀₀ = 440 − 352 = 88 V
Since 88 V < 220 V (rated)
∴ 100 W bulb will not fuse.

output.text:
Given below are two statements:

Statement I: In mosses, the sporophyte is more elaborate than that in liverworts.

Statement II: The sporophyte in mosses is differentiated into a foot, seta and capsule, but not in case of liverworts.

In the light of the above statements, choose the correct answer from the options given below:

options.text: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct.
Statement I is correct but Statement II is incorrect.
Statement I is incorrect but Statement II is correct.

input.raw_text_snippet:
166. How many of the following are correct statements 
as per the situation. 
 
A. Vasa recta is highly developed in cortical 
nephrons. 
 
B. Tumor in renal pelvis is the condition of renal 
calculi. 
 
C. A person who has undergone prolonged fasting 
will have ketone bodies in his urine. 
 
D. A person who is not taking food or beverages 
will have less urea in urine. 
 
E. 
If a person goes to antarctica like cold place, 
ADH level increases. 
 
(1) One 
 
 
(2) Two 
 
(3) Three 
 
(4) Four

output.text:
How many of the following are correct statements as per the situation.

A. Vasa recta is highly developed in cortical nephrons.

B. Tumor in renal pelvis is the condition of renal calculi.

C. A person who has undergone prolonged fasting will have ketone bodies in his urine.

D. A person who is not taking food or beverages will have less urea in urine.

E. If a person goes to antarctica like cold place, ADH level increases.
output.explanation:
Vasa recta is highly reduced or absent in cortical nephrons.
Renal calculi = Kidney stones, formed by crystallization of salts in urine. Tumor in renal pelvis = Renal carcinoma or malignancy, not stones.
During prolonged fasting, fat is broken down for energy. This produces ketone bodies. Ketones appear in urine — a condition called ketonuria.
Urea is a product of protein metabolism. If no food is taken → less protein intake → less amino acid breakdown → less urea produced and excreted. Also, dehydration can reduce urine volume.
In cold environments, sweating reduces, and fluid loss is minimal. This suppresses ADH release → more dilute urine. Therefore, ADH decreases, not increases. (NCERT Ed. 2025-26, 11th, Page No. 208)

15.
A disc of radius 0.4 m and mass 1 kg rotates about
an axis passing through its centre and perpendicular
to its plane. The angular acceleration is 
2
10 rad/s . 
The tangential force applied to the rim of the disc is 
(1)
2 N
(2) 4 N
(3)
3 N
(4) 1 N

output.text: A disc of radius 0.4 m and mass 1 kg rotates about an axis passing through its centre and perpendicular to its plane. The angular acceleration is 10 rad/s². The tangential force applied to the rim of the disc is
output.explanation:
Given:
radius (r) = 0.4 m, Mass (m) = 1 kg
Angular acceleration (α) = 10 rad/s²
Tangential force (F) = ?
Moment of inertia of disc I = (mr²) / 2
I = (1 × (0.4)²) / 2
I = 0.08 kg m²
∴ Torque, τ = Iα
τ = 0.08 × 10 = 0.8 N m
τ = Fr ⇒ F = τ / r = 0.8 / 0.4 = 2 N

output.text: Figure shows the position-time (x – t) graph of a particle of mass 2 kg. What is the impulse at t = 4 s?
output.explanation: From 0–4 s, slope = 8/4 = 2 m/s ⇒ v = 2 m/s. For t > 4, x is constant ∴ v = 0. Impulse J = Δp = 2(0 - 2) = -4 Ns (opposite to motion).

15. 
A uniformly charged non-conducting solid sphere 
of radius R  has an electric potential of 80 V at its 
surface. What is the electric potential at a point 
located at a distance 
2
R
r =
 from the center of the 
sphere? (Assume potential at infinity is zero). 
 
(1) 120 V 
(2) 40 V 
 
(3) 110 V 
(4) 100 V

output.text: A uniformly charged non-conducting solid sphere of radius R has an electric potential of 80 V at its surface. What is the electric potential at a point located at a distance r = R/2 from the center of the sphere? (Assume potential at infinity is zero).
output.explanation:
V(r) = (kQ / 2R³)(3R² − r²)
The potential at the surface (r = R) is given by:
V_surface = kQ / R = 80 V
V_internal = (kQ / 2R³) [3R² − (R / 2)²]
V_internal = (kQ / 2R³) [3R² − R² / 4]
V_internal = (kQ / R) × 11/8
V_internal = 80 × 11/8 = 110 V

output.text: Trisomy of a sex chromosome is observed in:
output.explanation:
Klinefelter’s syndrome has the karyotype 47, XXY, which is a trisomy of sex chromosomes. Turner’s syndrome is monosomy (45, XO). Down’s syndrome is trisomy of an autosome (chromosome 21). Myotonic dystrophy is an autosomal dominant disorder, not a chromosomal trisomy.

output.text:
Given below are two statements: one is labelled as Assertion A and the other is labelled as Reason R:

Assertion A: The process of micturition is under the control of the central nervous system.

Reason R: Stretch receptors in the urinary bladder wall send signals to the CNS when the bladder fills, which then initiates contraction of bladder muscles and relaxation of the urethral sphincter.

In the light of the above statements, choose the correct answer from the options given below:

options.text: A is true but R is false.
A is false but R is true.
Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A.

output.explanation:
Micturition (urine release) is controlled by a reflex that involves:
• Stretch receptors detecting fullness of the bladder.
• CNS receiving the signal and sending motor commands.
• Coordinated muscle contraction and sphincter relaxation
This entire micturition reflex is regulated by the CNS, making the assertion and reason both true and related. (NCERT Ed. 2025-26, 11th, Page No. 213)

input.raw_text_snippet:
171. Which of the following substances exit from the 
renal tubule and collecting duct to help establish and 
maintain the osmotic concentration gradient in the 
renal medullary interstitium? 
 
(1) Sodium chloride and urea 
 
(2) Glucose and amino acids 
 
(3) Bicarbonate and water 
 
(4) Creatinine and hydrogen ions

output.text: Which of the following substances exit from the renal tubule and collecting duct to help establish and maintain the osmotic concentration gradient in the renal medullary interstitium?
output.explanation: The medullary interstitial gradient is vital for the concentration of urine. NaCl is actively transported out of the ascending limb of Henle's loop. Urea diffuses out of the medullary collecting ducts into the interstitium. Together, NaCl and urea create a hyperosmotic environment in the medulla, drawing water out of the descending limb and collecting duct (under ADH influence), concentrating urine.

List-II

or laminarin 
(C) Porphyra

by gemmae 
(D) Marchantia (IV) Oogamous sexual 
reproduction 
accompanied by complex 
post-fertilisation 
developments 
 
Choose the most appropriate answer from the 
options given below. 
 
(1) A-I, B-II, C-IV, D-III 
 
(2) A-III, B-IV, C-II, D-I 
 
(3) A-IV, B-III, C-II, D-I 
 
(4) A-II, B-IV, C-I, D-III

output.text:
Match List-I with List-II.

List-I
(A) Chara
(B) Fucus
(C) Porphyra
(D) Marchantia

List-II
(I) Contains pyrenoids
(II) Store food as mannitol or laminarin
(III) Reproduces asexually by gemmae
(IV) Oogamous sexual reproduction accompanied by complex post-fertilisation developments

Choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below.
output.explanation: Most of the members of chlorophyceae have one or more storage bodies called pyrenoids located in the chloroplasts. Pyrenoids contain protein besides starch. Some commonly found green algae are: Chlamydomonas, Volvox, Ulothrix, Spirogyra and Chara. Food is stored as complex carbohydrates, which may be in the form of laminarin or mannitol in case of brown algae. The common forms are Ectocarpus, Dictyota, Laminaria, Sargassum and Fucus. Sexual reproduction is oogamous and accompanied by complex post fertilisation developments in red algae. The common members are: Polysiphonia, Porphyra, Gracilaria and Gelidium. Asexual reproduction in liverworts takes place by fragmentation of thalli, or by the formation of specialised structures called gemmae e.g.; Marchantia.

input.raw_text_snippet:
175. Read the following statements. 
 
A. The medullary region of the kidney is organized 
into several conical structures known as 
medullary pyramids, which project into the 
calyces. 
 
B. The cortical region of the kidney extends 
between adjacent medullary pyramids in the 
form of renal pelvis. 
 
C. A glomerulus together with its surrounding 
Bowman’s capsule is referred to as a renal 
corpuscle. 
 
D. The renal corpuscle, proximal convoluted 
tubule (PCT), and distal convoluted tubule 
(DCT) are all located within the cortical region 
of the kidney. 
 
How many of the statements given above are 
incorrect? 
 
(1) One 
(2) Two 
 
(3) Three 
(4) Four

output.text:
Read the following statements.

A. The medullary region of the kidney is organized into several conical structures known as medullary pyramids, which project into the calyces.

B. The cortical region of the kidney extends between adjacent medullary pyramids in the form of renal pelvis.

C. A glomerulus together with its surrounding Bowman’s capsule is referred to as a renal corpuscle.

D. The renal corpuscle, proximal convoluted tubule (PCT), and distal convoluted tubule (DCT) are all located within the cortical region of the kidney.

How many of the statements given above are incorrect?
output.explanation: Medullary pyramids are conical structures projecting into minor calyces. The cortical extensions between pyramids are called renal columns, not renal pelvis. The renal pelvis is a funnel-like structure that collects urine. Glomerulus + Bowman’s capsule = renal corpuscle. Renal corpuscle, PCT, and DCT lie in the cortex of the kidney.

33. 
A cyclist skids (both wheels locked) for 5 m before 
coming to a stop. During this process, the force on 
the cycle due to the road is 200 N and is directly 
opposed to the motion. The work done by the cycle 
on the road is: 
 
(1) 1000 J 
(2) –1000 J 
 
(3) –500 J 
(4) zero

output.text: A cyclist skids (both wheels locked) for 5 m before coming to a stop. During this process, the force on the cycle due to the road is 200 N and is directly opposed to the motion. The work done by the cycle on the road is:
output.explanation: Work on a body = force × displacement of that body's point of application. The road doesn't move, so despite the 200 N friction, the road's displacement is zero ⇒ work done by the cycle on the road is 0.

23.
Three bars AB, BC and CD of equal lengths and
equal area of cross section are connected in series.
Their thermal conductivities are in the ratio KAB :
KBC : KCD = 2:4:3. If the open ends of the first and
last bar are at temperature 200 C

 and 18 C

respectively, then in the steady state the temperature
of the 1st Junction (B) will be
(1)
96° C
(2)
116° C
(3)
129° C
(4)
132° C

output.text: Three bars AB, BC and CD of equal lengths and equal area of cross section are connected in series. Their thermal conductivities are in the ratio KAB : KBC : KCD = 2 : 4 : 3. If the open ends of the first and last bar are at temperature 200°C and 18°C respectively, then in the steady state the temperature of the 1st Junction (B) will be
output.explanation:
Given,
Length & area are equal i.e.
AB = BC = CD = L
(A)_{AB} = (A)_{BC} = (A)_{CD} = A
And thermal conductivity,
(K)_{AB} = 2K, (K)_{BC} = 4K, (K)_{CD} = 3K
Equivalent thermal conductivity of the given series,
3L / (K_{eq} A) = L / (2KA) + L / (4KA) + L / (3KA)
K_{eq} = 36K / 13
(Q)_{AD} = (Q)_{AB}
K_{eq} · A (200 - 18) / 3L = 2K · A (200 - θ) / L
(36K / 13) × (200 - 18) / 3 = 2 (200 - θ)
(6 / 13) × 182 = 200 - θ
θ = 116°C
So, the temperature at B, θ = 116°C

input.raw_text_snippet:
163.  
 
 
 
Carefully observe the graph showing osmolarity (in 
mOsmol/L) along different regions of the nephron 
and collecting duct. Identify how many of the 
following statements are correct: 
 
A. The osmolarity of the filtrate remains nearly 
constant in the PCT and the early descending 
limb. 
 
B. The tip of the loop of Henle exhibits the highest 
osmolarity (~1200 mOsm/L) due to water 
reabsorption and limited solute movement. 
 
C. Osmolarity decreases sharply in the ascending 
limb due to active transport of NaCl and 
impermeability to water. 
 
D. The distal convoluted tubule maintains a high 
osmolarity similar to that of the descending 
limb of LOH. 
 
E. 
In the collecting duct, osmolarity increases 
again due to ADH-mediated water reabsorption, 
especially in the presence of a hyperosmotic 
medullary interstitium. 
 
(1) Only three statements are correct 
 
(2) Only four statements are correct 
 
(3) All five statements are correct 
 
(4) Only two statements are correct

output.text:
Carefully observe the graph showing osmolarity (in mOsmol/L) along different regions of the nephron and collecting duct. Identify how many of the following statements are correct:

A. The osmolarity of the filtrate remains nearly constant in the PCT and the early descending limb.

B. The tip of the loop of Henle exhibits the highest osmolarity (~1200 mOsm/L) due to water reabsorption and limited solute movement.

C. Osmolarity decreases sharply in the ascending limb due to active transport of NaCl and impermeability to water.

D. The distal convoluted tubule maintains a high osmolarity similar to that of the descending limb of LOH.

E. In the collecting duct, osmolarity increases again due to ADH-mediated water reabsorption, especially in the presence of a hyperosmotic medullary interstitium.
output.explanation: PCT and early descending limb maintain ~300 mOsm/L; osmolarity doesn’t change significantly yet. The tip of Henle’s loop is the most concentrated (~1200 mOsm/L) due to water leaving the tubule. Osmolarity drops sharply in the ascending limb due to active Na⁺/Cl⁻ reabsorption and impermeability to water. DCT has low osmolarity, not high; reabsorption of salts continues, and it remains relatively dilute. ADH acts on the collecting duct to reabsorb water, concentrating the urine again, especially in the medullary region. (NCERT Ed. 2025-26, 11th, Page No. 211)

input.raw_text_snippet:
95.
Given below are two statements: one is labelled as
Assertion A and the other is labelled as Reason R:
Assertion A: The counter current mechanism in the
kidney helps to maintain an increasing osmolarity
gradient from the cortex to the inner medulla.
Reason R: The special arrangement of Henle’s loop
and vasa recta allows the exchange and recycling of
NaCl and ammonia, which maintains the medullary
osmotic gradient.
In the light of the above statements, choose the
correct answer from the options given below:
(1) A is true but R is false.
(2) A is false but R is true.
(3) Both A and R are true and R is the correct
explanation of A.
(4) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct
explanation of A.

output.text:
Given below are two statements: one is labelled as Assertion A and the other is labelled as Reason R:

Assertion A: The counter current mechanism in the kidney helps to maintain an increasing osmolarity gradient from the cortex to the inner medulla.

Reason R: The special arrangement of Henle’s loop and vasa recta allows the exchange and recycling of NaCl and ammonia, which maintains the medullary osmotic gradient.

In the light of the above statements, choose the correct answer from the options given below:

options.text: A is true but R is false.
A is false but R is true.
Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.

output.explanation: In the human kidney, the osmolarity increases from the outer cortex (~300 mOsmol/L) to the inner medulla (~1200 mOsmol/L). This gradient is established and maintained by the counter-current mechanism (involving the Henle's loop and vasa recta). The arrangement of Henle's loop and vasa recta is responsible for maintaining the gradient, the specific solutes involved are NaCl and Urea. Ammonia is secreted into the tubules to maintain pH and ionic balance, but it is not the solute recycled to maintain the hyperosmolarity of the medullary interstitium. The gradient is mainly caused by NaCl and urea.

Statement I is incorrect but Statement II is correct.
Both Statement I and Statement II are correct.

input.raw_text_snippet:
174. During hemodialysis process: 
 
I. 
Blood is drained from a convenient artery and 
anticoagulant is added (heparin). 
 
II. Removal of nitrogenous waste from blood. 
 
III. Blood is passed through a coiled porous 
cellophane membrane of tube bathing in 
dialysis fluid. 
 
IV. Blood is mixed with anti-heparin and passed 
into vein. 
 
(1) I → IV → III → II 
(2) I → II → III → IV 
 
(3) I → III → II → IV 
(4) I → II → IV → III

output.text:
During hemodialysis process:
I. Blood is drained from a convenient artery and anticoagulant is added (heparin).
II. Removal of nitrogenous waste from blood.
III. Blood is passed through a coiled porous cellophane membrane of tube bathing in dialysis fluid.
IV. Blood is mixed with anti-heparin and passed into vein.
output.explanation:
Correct sequence of steps during the hemodialysis process is:
I. Blood is drained from an artery and heparin is added to prevent clotting.
III. Blood is passed through a coiled, porous cellophane membrane surrounded by dialysis fluid for exchange of substances.
II. During this process, nitrogenous wastes (like urea, creatinine) diffuse out into the dialysis fluid.
IV. Clean blood is then mixed with anti-heparin and returned to a vein.

147. Which of the following correctly categorizes the 
substances into primarily actively and passively 
reabsorbed components during the processing of 
glomerular filtrate? 
 
Substances: 
Bicarbonate 
ions, 
amino 
acids, 
nitrogenous wastes, Na⁺, water: 
 
(1) Actively reabsorbed – Bicarbonate ions, Na⁺; 
Passively reabsorbed – Water, amino acids, 
nitrogenous wastes 
 
(2) Actively reabsorbed – Water, nitrogenous 
wastes, amino acids; Passively reabsorbed – 
Na⁺, bicarbonate ions 
 
(3) Actively reabsorbed – Water, Na⁺, nitrogenous 
wastes; Passively reabsorbed – Bicarbonate 
ions, amino acids 
 
(4) Actively reabsorbed – Bicarbonate ions, amino 
acids, Na⁺; Passively reabsorbed – Water,

output.text: Which of the following correctly categorizes the substances into primarily actively and passively reabsorbed components during the processing of glomerular filtrate? Substances: Bicarbonate ions, amino acids, nitrogenous wastes, Na⁺, water:

options.text: Actively reabsorbed – Bicarbonate ions, Na⁺; Passively reabsorbed – Water, amino acids, nitrogenous wastes
Actively reabsorbed – Water, nitrogenous wastes, amino acids; Passively reabsorbed – Na⁺, bicarbonate ions
Actively reabsorbed – Water, Na⁺, nitrogenous wastes; Passively reabsorbed – Bicarbonate ions, amino acids
Actively reabsorbed – Bicarbonate ions, amino acids, Na⁺; Passively reabsorbed – Water, nitrogenous wastes

output.explanation:
Actively Reabsorbed:
• Bicarbonate ions – to maintain pH balance.
• Amino acids – through carrier proteins via secondary active transport.
• Na⁺ – by active transport via sodium-potassium pumps.
Passively Reabsorbed:
• Water – follows solutes osmosis.
• Nitrogenous wastes (like urea) – partly reabsorbed by diffusion (passively).
(NCERT Ed. 2025-26, 11th, Page No. 209)

output.text: An air bubble of radius 1 cm rises from the bottom portion through a liquid of density 1.5 g/cc at a constant speed of 0.25 cm s⁻¹. If the density of air is neglected, the coefficient of viscosity of the liquid is approximately, (in Pa-s)
output.explanation:
Given, Radius of air bubble (r) = 0.01 m
Terminal velocity (v_T) = 0.25 × 10⁻² m/s
Density of liquid (ρ) = 1.5 × 10³ kg/m³
Density of air (σ_air) = 0, g = 9.8 m/s², η = ?
We know that, terminal velocity
(v_T) = (2r²(ρ − σ_air)g) / (9η)
or η = (2r²(ρ − σ_air)g) / (9v_T)
η = (2/9) × ((0.01)²(1.5 × 10³ − 0) × 9.8) / (0.25 × 10⁻²) = 130.667
η ≈ 130 Pa-s

27. 
A capacitor of capacitance C is charged to a 
potential V and disconnected. It is now connected to 
an uncharged capacitor of capacitance 2C. What is 
the percentage loss in stored energy?  
 
(1) 33.3% 
(2) 50%  
 
(3) 66.7% 
(4) 25%

output.text: A capacitor of capacitance C is charged to a potential V and disconnected. It is now connected to an uncharged capacitor of capacitance 2C. What is the percentage loss in stored energy?
output.explanation: Initial Energy, Uᵢ = ½ CV². Common potential, V_c = Q_total / C_total = (CV + 0) / (C + 2C) = V / 3. Final Energy, U_f = ½ (3C) V_c² = ½ (3C) (V² / 9) = 1/6 CV². ΔU = ½ CV² - 1/6 CV² = (3 - 1) / 6 CV² = 1/3 CV². Percentage Loss in energy, ΔU / Uᵢ × 100 = (1/3) / (1/2) × 100 = 2/3 × 100 = 66.67%.

output.text: The upper (2/3)rd part of a fixed inclined plane of inclination θ is perfectly smooth while lower (1/3)rd part is rough. A block starting from rest at the top of the plane, comes to rest just at the bottom. The coefficient of kinetic friction between the block and the lower (1/3)rd part of the plane is:
output.explanation: Net work done is zero (starts and ends at rest). Gravity work = mgLsinθ. Friction acts only on lower L/3: loss = μmgcosθ(L/3). Set equal ⇒ mgLsinθ = μmgcosθ(L/3) ⇒ μ = 3tanθ

output.text: Two blocks of masses 4 kg and 6 kg are connected at the two ends of a light inextensible string passing over a frictionless pulley. The accelerations of 4 kg and 6 kg blocks are ā₁ and ā₂ respectively. Then the value of |ā₁ – ā₂| is: (g = 10 m s⁻²)
output.explanation: For an ideal Atwood machine, a = (6 - 4)g / (6 + 4) = 2 m s⁻². The blocks have equal and opposite accelerations, so ā₂ = -ā₁ ⇒ |ā₁ - ā₂| = |2ā₁| = 2 · 2 = 4 m s⁻².

Identify the correct statements about sterilization methods:
A. They are generally advised as a terminal method to prevent further pregnancies.
B. In vasectomy, a small part of the fallopian tube is removed or tied up.

output.text: Which component of the immune system is primarily responsible for the graft rejection?

options.text: Humoral immune response mediated by B-lymphocytes.
Cell-mediated immune response mediated by T-lymphocytes.
Physiological barriers like acid in the stomach and tears.
Innate immunity present at the time of birth.

output.explanation: Graft rejection mainly occurs due to the cell-mediated immune response. T-lymphocytes recognize the transplanted organ as non-self and attack it. Cytotoxic T cells are especially responsible for destroying graft cells.

153. The given diagram shows the skulls of two different 
mammals. 
 
Which of the following correctly describes the 
difference between these skulls? 
 
(1) Skull B is the ancestor of skull A. 
 
(2) Skull A has more brain capacity than skull B. 
 
(3) Skull A is of a human and skull B is of an ape. 
 
(4) Skull A is of an adult chimpanzee and skull B 
is of a human.

output.text:
The given diagram shows the skulls of two different mammals.

Which of the following correctly describes the difference between these skulls?

options.text: Skull B is the ancestor of skull A.
Skull A has more brain capacity than skull B.
Skull A is of a human and skull B is of an ape.
Skull A is of an adult chimpanzee and skull B is of a human.

output.explanation: Skull A is of an adult chimpanzee and skull B is of a human.

output.text: A lift starts ascending from rest with constant acceleration of 2 m/s², then with constant velocity and finally comes to rest with constant retardation of 2 m/s². If total time taken is 10 s and total distance covered is 32 m, then maximum speed with which lift travels, is:
output.explanation: Lift ascends with acceleration 2 m s⁻², then constant velocity, then retardation 2 m s⁻². Total time = 10 s, total distance = 32 m. Maximum speed = 6 m s⁻¹.
